title: Shark Friends and the Deep Deep Dark
summary: Dimitar and Seeker brew a magical concoction using unusual ingredients like feathers and maple syrup, which transports them to the Deep Deep Dark of the ocean. Exploring with special diving helmets, they meet a friendly Whale Shark and a Cookie Cutter Shark before encountering a Blackbelly Lanternshark who has lost his bioluminescent glow. Feeling lost and sad without his light, the shark receives help from the Mergirls and a red flashlight. When kelp fails to hold the light in place, a sturdy pipe cleaner saves the day. After a brief meeting with a miniature Pocket Shark, the two friends use a 'go-home potion' to return safely to Seeker's castle.

highlightQuote.text: We need to see each other to stick together.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: This one's about Dimitar and Seeker.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It starts off with Seeker. Someone that starts with S.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Sam?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, no Seeker.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Once upon a time, Seeker was out for a walk.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I need this to be...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Seeker was out for a walk outside his house.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Ding dong ding dong.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And he heard the doorbell of his house so he ran back to his house and he looked and saw who was ringing his doorbell.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Ding dong, it was Dimitar.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh hi Dimitar. I was just going for a walk, you want to come with me?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, I want to do a potion. Now I'm not allergic to coral anymore so I can do gemstone coral reef.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that sounds like fun. You know, I was experimenting with some new potion ingredients. Do you remember what they were, Izzy? What were those new ingredients?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Feathers, maple syrup, sugar, cotton balls.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And a little water, yeah. Feathers, maple syrup, sugar, and cotton balls. And I cooked it up Dimitar and it smelled really good but it looked really yucky. And I think if we drink it like a magic potion it might take us someplace interesting but I have no idea where.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh and I don't feel... and you had to add feathers and apples, and red apples.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Red, oh. And Dimitar said 'Ah that's very interesting. I didn't know you could make new potions like that. Where do you think it would take us?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Let's try it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker said 'Yeah, let's try it. Okay, come on in Dimitar.' And so they went into Seeker's house and they opened up the door to the potion closet.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hello. Daddy.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Kiss, Izzy. I love you.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I love you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker pulled out the brand new potion he had just made that day. And they picked up the bottle. And first of all, what did the potion look like?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It looked like just brown mud.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh yeah, it got a little mucky because they boiled all those ingredients together. But what did it smell like?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It smelled like mud.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It smelled like mud, but mud with like a little maple syrup. And so Seeker said 'I know it doesn't look or smell great, but I bet it'll take us someplace interesting. Would you be willing to make up the rhyme this time after we take a sip?' Okay, so they both took a sip.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Fishta-pish, fishta-pish, fishta-pish.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Fishta-pish. And then, ker-sploosh, they found themselves in a new place they'd never been before called the Deep Deep Dark.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then someone swam up to them, who was very friendly. Hello, my name is Whale Shark. I am very friendly. You can even swim with me.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker said 'Oh my goodness.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And I am very friendly.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It's a good thing when we appeared down here in the Deep Deep Dark, we both have special diving helmets so we can breathe, right Dimitar? And Dimitar said 'Oh my goodness, I've never been underwater like this, it's amazing. I wonder if we could eat some of these fish, they look delicious.' And Seeker said 'No, I think this Whale Shark wants to be our friend, don't you?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes I do.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And the Whale Shark was very big. Seeker and Dimitar are kind of small and the Whale Shark was much bigger than them. But they decided to follow the Whale Shark and see where that adventure would take them.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then, and then someone swam up to them with the Whale Shark's tail, and he said 'My name is Cookie Cutter Shark.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Cookie Cutter Shark. Oh my, said Seeker. Do you eat cookies?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No. I eat fishy, fishy fishy.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Fishy fishy. And I see you have a round mouth with shark teeth. Do you take bites that look like cookies?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, circular bites.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I see. And Whale Shark, what do you think of Cookie Cutter Shark?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: He's my friend.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh I'm glad to hear that. But what...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then someone swam up to them, Hammerhead. My name is Hammerhead.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh hi Hammerhead, said Seeker. And Dimitar said 'What's that?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm a shark. I have also got my friend named... Daddy, I just need to look at something for a minute.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what are you going to look at?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: This book. So this is Cookie Cutter Shark, and then this is... I just need some information about these sharks. Um, it says... Daddy, I just need a name of a shark. I forgot a name of a shark.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Which one?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was this one, this one.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well that's the Blackbelly Lanternshark. The glow in the dark tummies. And it says 'We need to see each other so we can stick together.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: So the Hammerhead Shark said 'My friend is the Blackbelly Lanternshark, and and he says something that's really funny. He says something that's really funny. Um, it's called 'We need to see each other to stick together.' That's what he said.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: But then another Blackbelly Lanternshark swam up. And then he said 'I have a problem. My lantern went out.' And it was true, the other Lanternshark's belly was glowing, but this one was all dark.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then she said... Do you want to hear something? I've got a very good thing to say. It's called 'We need to see each other to stick together.' That's what the book says about sharks sharks sharks. In the book called Sharks Sharks Sharks. And and there's some fish like you but none of them they were both glowing in the page and one said a very silly word, it's called 'We need to see each other to stick together.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And that one Lanternbelly Shark... oh no Blackbelly... what are they called again?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Blackbelly Lanternshark.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: So that one Lanternshark that had lost his glow was very sad because he said 'I got lost. I couldn't find the other Lanternsharks because my belly stopped glowing and they couldn't see me.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um I can help you, I can help you. Here, here's my very bright flashlight, it's red. Here's my red flashlight. I can turn it on for you. Here.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, thank you. That's a very nice flashlight. But can I keep it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Of course.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well thank you, because normally I just have my belly shining all the time so that the other sharks can see me, but I'll have to keep this flashlight on all the time. But wait, there's a problem. I can't hold it because I'm just a shark. I don't have hands.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Okay, then then I can just call somebody that lives underwater but has very good things for holding.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, good things for holding. Who lives underwater who has good things for... Seeker said 'Hmm I wonder who we could call' and Dimitar said 'I don't know anybody...'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mergirls! The Mergirls are, they're just girls with tails. Yeah and sometimes they appear here.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, hello Mergirls, said Seeker. Can you help us out?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, and the Mergirls say 'We live underwater.' Lanternfish with no, with no glow. Um we're the Mergirls, the three Mergirls. One of us is blue, one of us is yellow, and one of us is pink.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, nice to meet you. Well welcome to the Deep Deep Dark.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And and one of us is rainbow.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I see. And I just need help holding this flashlight because I don't have hands like you.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, a Mergirl, the pink Mergirl says 'I can carry the flashlight around.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you'll just come around with me all the time? And then light the way so the other sharks can see me? Thank you.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, um or maybe... I live this far down, I live this far down because I like these sharks.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, and Rainbow Mergirl said 'You know, I do have some kelp. We live in our cave, we live in our cave. Our cave is on the sea floor and it's easy for us to get up to the Deep Deep Dark. We just swim. But we have hands.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well thank you so much for offering to help. And I wonder if there's some way we could attach the flashlight to me. And the Rainbow Mergirl said 'You know, maybe we could try tying it on with some kelp.' And so they tried, but the flashlight slipped away.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then Isabella said 'Try this pipe cleaner.' This pipe cleaner's really good for twisting, and it stays together really well.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so they attached the flashlight with a pipe cleaner to the poor Lanternshark with no glow. So now he had the flashlight glow and he also had new friends, which were the Mergirls who could help him out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker and Dimitar said 'Wow, the Deep Deep Dark is an amazing place.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Until someone swam over to them and said 'Ho ho, I am one more shark who you haven't met yet.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, who are you?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I am Pocket Shark. Yeah, that's why I can fit in most pockets because... and I got a name called Pocket Shark because I have two original pockets near my fins.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I see. Would you like to come back to my castle? said Seeker.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, of course, because I need to go look for food soon.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Dimitar says 'You know what? Me too. Let's go have lunch.' And so Seeker and Dimitar took the go-home potion and bloop, they were back at Seeker's castle. Wet, but happy.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Wet but happy.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And that's the end of our story.

characters.name: Seeker
characters.description: A brave dwarven explorer and mediator, he resides in a glowing castle hub—a multiversal dock for skyships anchored within the skyline of New York City. From this urban sanctum, he conducts silver slime experiments and tends to wandering cats amidst a massive library. Frequently swapping between a red coat and a mystical purple cloak, his braided red beard remains his most eccentric tool, capable of catching small fish. A master of fusion, he combines ancient stone-craft with high-velocity mechanics, utilizing a legendary magic ladder to scale the shifting architecture of the realms. Whether brewing portal potions, harvesting floof-berries in Limbo with gemstone goggles, or aiding Buddy the Mouse, this walnut-loving student of the stars remains the hero who orchestrated the daring rescue of the world.

characters.name: Dimitar
characters.description: A sharp-witted resident of a circular island castle, this two-century-old purple dragonet carries a pirate’s salt-crusted legacy and a fierce devotion to his salvaged library. Formerly of the Cadasori galleries, Seeker’s grumpy companion now roams the deep to save his ancestral shores, though a recent volcanic disturbance has left him uncharacteristically sickened and frail. Driven by habit and a tireless appetite for fish, he bickers incessantly over snacks while guarding his remaining volumes with a brooding, protective intensity.

characters.name: Whale Shark
characters.description: A very large and friendly shark who welcomes Seeker and Dimitar to the Deep Deep Dark.

characters.name: Cookie Cutter Shark
characters.description: A shark with a round mouth that takes circular bites and considers the Whale Shark a friend.

characters.name: Hammerhead Shark
characters.description: A distinct shark encountered underwater who is friends with the Lanternsharks.

characters.name: Blackbelly Lanternshark
characters.description: A species of shark with a glowing belly; one individual lost his light and became separated from his group.

characters.name: Blue Mergirl
characters.description: One of the Mergirls living in a cave on the sea floor.

characters.name: Yellow Mergirl
characters.description: A member of the Mergirls who live underwater.

characters.name: Pink Mergirl
characters.description: A compassionate spirit of the deep, she serves as a beacon for sharks denied a natural glow, illuminating their path through the abyss before retreating to her grotto to find cozy comfort in the embrace of a plush seal stuffy.

characters.name: Rainbow Mergirl
characters.description: A resourceful mergirl known for her clever use of kelp to aid the Lanternshark, she balances her practical ingenuity with a spirited streak, recently exemplified by a sharp disagreement with the Pink Mergirl over their cherished stuffed animals.

characters.name: Pocket Shark
characters.description: A tiny shark with pockets near his fins who can fit inside most people's pockets.

characters.name: Isabella
characters.description: A towering Dimatar scholar and alchemist of Atsukeeseet, Mama Izzy is a clever kiddo at heart and a steadfast guardian of rooftop gardens and intricate dollhouses. Gifted with the power of flight, she navigates the open heights with aquatic grace, her massive frame often shielded by a special protective metal that glimmers even as she descends into the shadows. As a resourceful quartermaster, she employs red flashlights, gold-laced slimes, and pipe cleaners to mend a Lanternshark’s failing light, yet her generosity remains her hallmark; she freely shares her vast library, brokers homes for dragons, and teaches others to find the resonance in their own voices. Her most profound magic resides in her vast, soft blanket—a sanctuary of ultimate coziness—where she remains a brave, quick-witted protector to all who honor her singular request for absolute silence.

places.name: Seeker's House
places.description: Perched at the edge of a mountain, this warm stone sanctuary—Seeker’s original residence and whimsical home shared with Uncle Crisco—has evolved from a secret vault into the purring, chaotic heart of a massive feline gala. Within its well-lit halls where Muppets gather for snacks and scientific discussions, lies a special hidden closet where Seeker brews an enchanted collection of potions that shimmer in every imaginable color.

places.name: Potion Closet
places.description: Hidden behind a secret door in Seeker’s castle lies a vaulted chamber lined with enchanted cabinets, rare ingredients, and complex concoctions. Shimmering vials containing the molten heat of Volcano World and the verdant gales of Grassy World glow upon the shelves, while the air remains thick with curious sniffs—fleeting, sensory echoes of the bottled magic stored within this hidden sanctum.

places.name: Gemstone Coral Reef
places.description: A place Dimitar wants to visit now that he is no longer allergic to coral.

places.name: Deep Deep Dark
places.description: A dark, mysterious underwater realm filled with unique sharks and mergirls.

places.name: Mergirls' Cave
places.description: A home on the sea floor where the Mergirls live.

places.name: Seeker's Castle
places.description: Behind an unassuming facade and a solitary doorbell lies Seeker’s home, a sprawling magical fortress that serves as a vital sanctuary for weary legends. The interior expands into a labyrinthine refuge where an expansive library of mystical books towers beside a potion closet that acts as the true gateway to adventure. This fortress is a hub for calls to action and well-earned rest, where Wobbly Whiskers waits to greet the Seeker and Buddy the Mouse—though the warmth of their homecoming is inevitably punctuated by his latest sticky mess upon the rug.
